sections.paragraph: δίδραχμον, ου, τό [δίς, δραχμή] double drachma, the amount of money totaling the value of a δίδραχμον, a coin worth two drachmas but no longer in circulation in NT times, and equivalent to two days’ wages for a day laborer; the total of various coins, conventionally understood as a δ., constituted the annual temple maintenance tax levied on male Israelites Mt 17:24.
